_meta.description: Washington State (Seattle metro) water quality data for PlumbLead filtration play
_meta.notes: PNW water is naturally very soft. The play here is filtration (chlorine, PFAS, lead), NOT softeners.

region_profile.name: Greater Seattle / Puget Sound
region_profile.water_character: Very soft, low mineral, surface water sourced
region_profile.NOT_a_concern: hardness — do NOT pitch softeners in Seattle metro
region_profile.disinfection_note: Seattle uses chlorine (not chloramine). Some suburban systems use chloramine. Carbon filters handle both but sizing differs.

seattle.name: Seattle
seattle.source: Cedar River + Tolt River watersheds (protected mountain surface water)
seattle.pfas_status: Not detected in Cedar/Tolt sources (2023 testing). One emergency well (Blvd Park) showed PFOA at 5.7 ppt — above new EPA MCL of 4 ppt. Wells rarely used.
seattle.lead_risk: Elevated — many pre-1960 homes with lead solder joints. Seattle has oldest housing stock in PNW.
seattle.housing_age_note: 35%+ of Seattle housing built before 1960. Lead solder common until 1986.

bellevue.name: Bellevue
bellevue.pfas_status: Testing ongoing per EPA mandate. Results pending for some sources.
bellevue.lead_risk: Moderate — newer housing stock than Seattle but some older neighborhoods

tacoma.name: Tacoma
tacoma.pfas_status: Tacoma has industrial history — PFAS awareness higher. Some well sources more vulnerable.
tacoma.lead_risk: Elevated — significant pre-1960 housing stock, former industrial city

renton.name: Renton
renton.pfas_status: Industrial areas near Boeing — elevated PFAS awareness

redmond.name: Redmond
redmond.pfas_status: Low risk — newer tech campus area

kent.name: Kent
kent.pfas_status: Industrial valley — higher risk. Boeing, aerospace manufacturing nearby.

everett.name: Everett
everett.pfas_status: Paine Field / Boeing area — PFAS contamination documented in some groundwater

whole_house_carbon.label: Whole House Carbon Filter
whole_house_carbon.sell_trigger: Do you notice a chlorine taste or smell when you run the tap?

under_sink_ro.label: Under-Sink Reverse Osmosis
under_sink_ro.sell_trigger: Are you concerned about PFAS or lead in your drinking water?

lead_filter.label: Lead Reduction Filter (POU)
lead_filter.sell_trigger: When was your home built? Homes before 1986 likely have lead solder joints.

combo_carbon_ro.label: Complete Protection Package
combo_carbon_ro.sell_trigger: Want clean water from every tap AND pure drinking water? This is the full solution.

pfas_education.what: PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances) are man-made 'forever chemicals' found in nonstick cookware, food packaging, firefighting foam, and many consumer products.
pfas_education.epa_rule: EPA finalized MCLs for 6 PFAS compounds in April 2024. PFOA and PFOS set at 4 parts per trillion. Water systems must comply by 2029.
pfas_education.removal: Reverse osmosis removes 90-99% of PFAS. Activated carbon (GAC) removes some but not all PFAS types. RO is the gold standard for point-of-use PFAS removal.
pfas_education.homeowner_angle: Even if your municipal water tests clean, PFAS can enter from household products, and source water quality can change. Point-of-use RO provides a permanent safety net.
pfas_education.fear_factor: PFAS are linked to cancer, thyroid disease, immune system effects, and reproductive issues. Parents with young children are the most motivated buyers.

lead_education.source: Lead in drinking water almost always comes from the home's plumbing, not the water supply. Lead solder was used in copper pipe joints until it was banned in 1986.
lead_education.risk_homes: Homes built before 1986 — especially before 1960 — are at highest risk.
lead_education.testing: Simple first-draw water test ($20-40 kit or lab test) reveals lead levels. EPA action level is 15 ppb.
lead_education.solution: Point-of-use certified lead filter or RO system. NSF/ANSI 53 certification required for lead reduction claims.
lead_education.sell_approach: Non-alarmist: 'Most homes in this neighborhood were built in [decade]. Standard practice was lead solder. A $300 filter gives you peace of mind.'
